body.title: [triage] Ticket becomes a chart: each pipeline station appends declared-vs-actual so later stations can reconcile
body.intention: I intend that a ticket carries a longitudinal record — the hospital patient-chart model — where every pipeline station appends what it DECLARED and what it ACTUALLY did, so a downstream station (running in a different agent with no shared context) can reconcile against upstream declarations.
body.description:
As the dev process pipelines into independent STATIONS / agents (D-dev-pipeline-stations; examiner-station), a late step (e.g. the post-build reconciliation, T-post-build-diff-reconciles-declared) no longer has the earlier steps' declarations in its context — they ran in another agent. The data has to travel WITH the work. Akien's framing: the ticket becomes what a hospital calls the record that tracks everything done with a patient along the way — a CHART (patient record / EHR). Each station writes its entry.

Shape: add a structured `chart` (a.k.a. provenance ledger) to the ticket body — an append-only list of station entries, each ~{station, at, declared: {...}, actual: {...}}. Examples: the pre-code/plan station writes declared Affected-files + intended behavior; the build station writes the actual staged diff (changed files, commit sha); the proof station writes the proof id; the close station writes the disposition. Downstream stations READ prior entries to reconcile (declared-vs-actual) instead of re-deriving from a context they don't have.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/ticket_store.py (the `chart` field on the body + append helper — envelope, edit body.*); the station skills that should write entries (/sprint-ticket plan+build+proof+close, the audits) — wire each to append; a reader helper for the reconciliation check.
**Design rules:** external-state-principle (state lives on the ticket, not in an agent's head — the KnightlyBuilder principle); homogeneity-over-special-case (one chart shape, every station writes the same way); docs-live-in-code; no-sqlite (flat-file ticket store only).
**Scope boundary:** IN — the chart data structure on the ticket + the append/read helpers + wiring the EXISTING close/build/audit stations to write entries. OUT — building new pipeline stations (that's the dev-pipeline work); not a schema migration of closed tickets (forward-looking; new/in-flight tickets grow a chart).
**Test plan:** after a claim -> build -> audit -> close cycle on a synthetic ticket, its chart has one entry per station with declared+actual populated; a reconciliation reader can retrieve the plan station's declared Affected-files from the chart without any live context. Round-trip through ticket_store preserves the chart (append-only, no clobber).
**Rollback:** the chart is an additive optional body field; readers tolerate its absence (fail-soft). Remove the field + append calls to revert.
**Logging:** log each chart append (station + entry keys) at INFO — it IS an interface crossing (station boundary).
**Observability:** `cc_queue.py show <id>` surfaces the chart; the running record of declared-vs-actual per station is greppable on the ticket file.
**Depends on:** relates D-dev-pipeline-stations + examiner-station; unblocks the pipelined (cross-agent) form of T-post-build-diff-reconciles-declared. Left triage pending Akien's confirmation of the chart shape before build.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
